cards.front: What is a default function argument in C++?
cards.back: A value that is automatically assigned to a function parameter if it is not provided in the function call

cards.front: How do default function arguments improve code readability?
cards.back: By making it clear what value is used when a parameter is not provided, reducing the need for comments

cards.front: Can default function arguments be used in function overloading?
cards.back: Yes, they can be used to distinguish between different function overloads

cards.front: Can default function arguments be used with function templates?
cards.back: Yes, they can be used to provide default values for template parameters

cards.front: What happens if a function has multiple default function arguments?
cards.back: Only the last default argument is used; the previous ones are ignored
